University of Auckland Consultancy Agreement
- 13 - Part 4
Terms and Conditions
PART 4 - TERMS AND CONDITIONS
1 CONDITIONS PRECEDENT
Agreement to Come into Effect
This Agreement is conditional upon satisfaction or waiver of the Conditions.
Conditions Precedent
The Conditions are conditions precedent and are inserted solely for the benefit of
Cancellation
If the Conditions are likely to not be satisfied by or the Consultant (as required
by the Schedule of Particulars) or waived by by the Conditional Date, then:
(a) may at any time before the Conditional Date require an extension of the
Conditional Date of up to 12 months;
(b) If any Condition is not satisfied, or waived by by the Conditional Date
or the extended Conditional Date (if any), may terminate this
Agreement by notice to the Consultant; and
(c) Upon termination of this Agreement under this clause, this Agreement shall
come to an end and neither party shall have any further claim against the other
arising under or in connection with that termination.
2 COMMENCEMENT / COMPLETION
Agreement Effective
This Agreement is effective from the Effective Date, notwithstanding the signing of this
Agreement on any other date.
Term
The Consultant shall provide the Services for the term identified in the Schedule of
Particulars or until the Agreement is earlier terminated.
Services Prior to Execution of this Agreement
Any Services provided prior to the date of this Agreement or satisfaction, or waiver of
the Conditions (whichever occurs latest) shall be deemed to have been provided under
and on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement, and any payments which
has agreed to make for the Services (or in relation to anything covered by the
Services) shall be treated as advanced payment of the Fee.
Extension of Term
may, at any time prior to the expiry of the term (if any) identified in the Schedule
of Particulars, serve notice on the Consultant requesting an extension of such term,
and the Consultant shall then negotiate such extension with in good faith.

University of Auckland Consultancy Agreement
- 14 - Part 4
Terms and Conditions
3 OBLIGATIONS OF CONTACT
Information
(a) shall provide to the Consultant (without charge) all information that is
available to and that is not prevented from disclosing due to
confidentiality requirements, and which appears to to be necessary and
not otherwise available to the Consultant, to enable the Consultant to provide
the Services.
(b) shall keep the Consultant informed on such matters as may appear to
to materially affect the Services.
Equipment and Facilities
(a) shall make the equipment and facilities described in the Schedule of
Particulars available to the Consultant, within a reasonable time of a request at
the cost agreed upon in the Schedule of Particulars, for the purpose of providing
the Services. The Consultant shall not be entitled to use any other equipment
or facilities of (including on the Site) except with the prior written
consent of
(b) does not warrant that any equipment and facilities supplied by it are
suitable for the intended work or of acceptable quality or condition. The
Consultant acknowledges that it uses equipment and facilities at its
own risk in all respects.
4 OBLIGATIONS OF THE CONSULTANT
Provision of the Services
The Consultant shall:
(a) promptly and diligently provide the Services in accordance with this Agreement;
(b) comply with:
(i) all of instructions in relation to the Services;
(ii) programme for provision of the Services (including using
reasonable endeavours to meet deadlines set by for provision of
the Services);
(c) assist with any assessment of:
(i) progress in the provision of the Services; and
(ii) payment of the Consultant as against the Schedule of Fees (if any);
(d) advise of the consequences of any changes in the programme; and
(e) advise and assist on all matters relating to the Services.

University of Auckland Consultancy Agreement
- 15 - Part 4
Terms and Conditions
Standard of Care
(a) Notwithstanding anything elsewhere in this Agreement, the Consultant
undertakes to that it has exercised and shall continue to exercise in the
provision of the Services the skill, care, diligence, prudence and foresight
ordinarily expected of a properly qualified and competent professional adviser
with the necessary resources and experience in providing services of a similar
nature, scope and complexity to the Services, having regard to best industry
practice both internationally and in New Zealand.
(b) The Consultant shall provide the Services in accordance with all applicable Law,
any contract related to the provision of the Services and any applicable listing
rules. If the Consultant becomes aware of any act or omission that will, or is
likely to, breach any Law, any contract related to the provision of the Services
or any applicable listing rules it shall as soon as reasonably practical notify
and shall follow instructions.
Authority of the Consultant
(a) The Consultant warrants that it has full legal capacity and authority to enter into
and complete this Agreement and that it has complied with all legal
requirements necessary for it to enter into a binding contract with
(b) The Consultant has no authority to act as agent on any matter, save
where expressly identified in Part 5 - The Services. The Consultant shall not be
regarded as and shall not hold itself out as being agent or partner for
any other purpose and shall not bind or attempt to commit the credit of
save as expressly authorised by this Agreement.
Presence on Site
The Consultant shall, with prior agreement, visit and be represented upon
the Site and visit all off-Site places of manufacture as often as may be necessary to
perform its obligations under this Agreement.
Information to be supplied by the Consultant
(a) The Consultant shall keep informed on all matters relating to the
Services, including, without limitation, details of all staff employed by the
Consultant in the provision of the Services.
(b) The Consultant shall promptly:
(i) supply to such information as reasonably requests,
including documentation containing sufficient instructions and detail for
to make full and proper use of the Services; and
(ii) answer all enquiries received from in relation to the Services.
(c) On termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Consultant shall, if required by
provide to all Documents and other information held by the
Consultant in relation to the supply of the Services.

Property
(a) shall:
(i) continue to have title to anything it supplies to the Consultant (including
any accessories to, or spare parts for, anything it supplies to the
Consultant); and
(ii) obtain title to anything it purchases from the Consultant on payment or
upon resolution if there is any dispute as to when a payment is due,
together “ Property”.
(b) Where practicable the Consultant shall mark any such property in its possession
or control as Property. When the Services are completed or
terminated the Consultant shall furnish inventories to of any of
Property that has not been consumed in the provision of the Services.
The Consultant shall deliver any of Property remaining upon
completion, expiry or termination as directed by
(c) Property shall be at the risk of the Consultant from the date it is
supplied to the Consultant or produced by the Consultant (as applicable) until:
(i) such property is delivered to or
(ii) such property is consumed in the provision of the Services in accordance
with this Agreement.
(d) The Consultant acknowledges that:
(i) may be deemed to have a security interest (for the purposes of
the Personal Property Securities Act 1999 (“PPSA”)) in
Property;
(ii) Part 9 of the PPSA shall not apply to that deemed security interest; and
(iii) may register a financing statement on the Personal Property
Securities Register in respect of that security interest.
(e) The Consultant shall:
(i) provide such information and assistance as may reasonably
require to register the financing statement, and waives its rights under
section 148 of the PPSA to receive a copy of the verification statement
receives; and
(ii) ensure that any other security interest it grants or has granted excludes
Property.
Co-operation

are from time to time employed by

(a) The Consultant shall retain all Documents from the Effective Date for the period
specified in the Schedule of Particulars, subject to clause 4.5(c).
(b) The Consultant acknowledges right to access such Documents and
shall allow its representatives and any authorised public officers to
inspect any Documents and shall answer queries or supply information
requested by them on reasonable notice.
(c) To the extent that any Documents are to be either or both created and
maintained on computer or other electronic storage device, then the Consultant
shall adhere to the procedure for backup of copies of those Documents notified
by
Approval of Documents
(a) The Consultant shall when so requested by submit for
approval, or amend, such of the Documents as may specify or require.
(b) No such approval shall affect the responsibility of the Consultant to provide the
Services or relieve it of its obligations under this Agreement.
Instructions to Others by Consultant
Not used.
Attendance at Meetings
The Consultant shall attend all meetings convened by to which it may be
summoned.
Health, Safety and Environment
(a) The Consultant shall, and shall ensure that all persons engaged by it (including
sub-contractors):
(i) comply with the requirements of the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015,
regulations made under that Act and any other relevant New Zealand
legislative requirements;
(ii) where required by undertake site hazard assessments and a
worksite safety plan in an approved format prior to commencement of
work and ensure any person performing work on their behalf is familiar
with these. The site hazard assessment and a worksite safety plan shall
be periodically updated if required by
(iii) immediately advise of any hazards or risks identified during the
course of the work, should there be risk of harm all work activities must
be suspended until appropriate controls are agreed and implemented;
(iv) immediately report all incidents and near misses to and actively
participate in Learning From Events processes as required;

(v) ensure that all areas over which it has control or at which it performs work
for including all equipment owned or operated, are in an orderly
state and used and maintained in a manner appropriate to avoid harm to
all persons, property and environment;
(vi) use only personnel who are qualified and appropriately trained for the
work being performed;
(vii) provide reporting on HSE performance as shall reasonably
determine;
(viii) ensure that all appropriate personal protective and safety equipment is
available and utilised whilst performing work for
(ix) undertake all HSE inductions and training in specific work control
procedures as required by
(x) at all times comply with all site signage;
(xi) comply with site instructions, HSE policies and procedures and
actively participate in HSE programmes, if relevant to the
Services, as notified by from time to time;
(xii) are not adversely affected by fatigue or by prescription medication, drugs
or alcohol and that they comply with Alcohol and Drugs policy,
a copy of which shall be provided to the Consultant.
(b) may prior to the start of any work by the Consultant or any persons
engaged by it (including sub-contractors), require the Consultant to develop and
submit for approval a HSE Management Plan in the form shall
advise. The Consultant and any persons engaged by it (including sub-
contractors) shall, at all times, work in compliance with the requirements of the
approved HSE Management Plan; and
(c) may monitor and audit the compliance of the Consultant or any persons
engaged by it (including sub-contractors) with the requirements of this clause.
Without prejudice to any other rights it may have, will discuss with the
Consultant any non-compliance that is identified. The Consultant will take all
reasonable steps to as soon as reasonably practicable remedy such non-
compliance.
Information Technology
(a) By mutual written agreement between the parties, the Consultant shall promptly
supply:
(i) reasonable support and maintenance services in relation to information
technology and other technology Deliverables (“IT Deliverables”) to be
agreed by the parties to ensure that can effectively utilise the IT
Deliverables and that the IT Deliverables remain error free and operate in
conformity with this Agreement; and

(ii) the source code in respect of any software provided to sufficient
to enable a reasonably competent programmer to maintain and modify the
IT Deliverables.
Reporting
The Consultant shall provide to a report at the frequency and containing the
details required by the Schedule of Particulars. The Consultant shall also provide any
other information described in the Schedule of Particulars at the frequency described
in the Schedule of Particulars.
5 PERSONNEL
Key Personnel
(a) The Consultant has appointed the persons identified in the Schedule of
Particulars as Key Personnel to carry out the functions respectively allocated to
them in the Schedule of Particulars. Such persons (or any replacement) shall
have full authority to act on behalf of the Consultant in accordance with their
respective functions, in respect of all matters connected with this Agreement.
(b) The Consultant shall not remove any such Key Personnel (or any replacements)
without the prior written consent of (such consent not to be
unreasonably withheld) unless such persons (or any replacements) become
incapacitated or are no longer in the employment of the Consultant. The
Consultant shall give notice forthwith on becoming aware of any such
event.
(c) Any replacement Key Personnel shall have experience and expertise
appropriate to carry out the work (which the Consultant shall demonstrate to
if requested in writing to do so including by providing their curricula vitae
and previous employer references) and shall be subject to the prior written
approval of (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).
Other Personnel
(a) Any other personnel provided by the Consultant shall be suitably qualified,
experienced and competent as required for the performance of the Consultant’s
obligations under this Agreement.
(b) The Consultant shall be responsible for all staff it engages to provide the
Services, whether or not employed directly by them.
Removal of Personnel
(a) shall be entitled after consultation with the Consultant to require the
removal from the provision of the Services of any person employed by the
Consultant whose performance or conduct in providing the Services is, in the
reasonable opinion of unsatisfactory. This right is subject to (i)
providing prior notice to the Consultant (where reasonably practicable) of any
concerns it has regarding performance of the Consultant’s Personnel; and (b)
the relevant Personnel being provided a reasonable opportunity to remedy their
performance or conduct.

(b) In the event that any Key Personnel (or any replacements) are removed under
clause 5.3(a), the Consultant shall at no cost to replace such person
with a person of experience and expertise appropriate to perform the functions
allocated to him whose identity shall be subject to the prior written approval of
(such approval not to be unreasonably withheld).
(c) In the event that any other personnel are removed under clause 5.3(a), the
Consultant shall at no cost to replace such person with a person of
experience and expertise appropriate to provide the Services.
Meeting with Staff
If calls on any individual member or members of the Consultant’s staff to
discuss, explain and or make presentations on any part of the Services, such individual
or individuals shall be readily available to do so on reasonable notice.
Representative
has nominated the person or persons specified in the Schedule of Particulars
to be its representative in respect of this Agreement. may from time to time
appoint alternative or further persons to so act and shall give the Consultant written
notice of such appointment. The Consultant shall not act on the instructions of any
other person. Any written approval given by Representative will be deemed
to be the approval of and will bind and the Consultant accordingly.
Consultant’s Representative
The person identified in the Schedule of Particulars as the Consultant’s representative
shall be available to receive all instructions from and shall be authorised by the
Consultant to act on its behalf in relation to all matters arising under this Agreement.
shall not be obliged to issue instructions to any other person. Any written
approval given by the Consultant’s representative will be deemed to be the approval of
the Consultant and will bind the Consultant accordingly.
6 ASSIGNMENT
Assignment or Subcontracting by the Consultant
The Consultant shall not assign, subcontract or transfer any right or obligation arising
out of, under or in connection with this Agreement to any person without prior
written consent. No such assignment, subcontract, transfer or consent by shall
in any way relieve the Consultant from any obligations or liability under this Agreement.
Consent of
(a) The consent of to the assignment, subcontracting or transfer of the
Consultant’s rights and obligations under this Agreement shall not be
unreasonably withheld or delayed, but may withhold its consent if
is not reasonably satisfied that the person to whom such assignment,
subcontract or transfer is proposed to be made:
(i) has the technical expertise necessary to perform its obligations under
this Agreement;

(iii) does not have any interests in real or apparent conflict with the provision
of the Services or interests generally.
(b) may as a condition of its approval of any sub-consultant require that
sub-consultant to execute a warranty in favour of in a form prepared
by

may, without the requirement for the consent of the Consultant, at any time by

7 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
Intellectual Property of Consultant
Intellectual Property of the Consultant existing at the Effective Date, including any
modification to such Intellectual Property effected without reference to
Intellectual Property, (“Consultant’s Intellectual Property”) shall remain the
property of the Consultant.
Intellectual Property of
(a) All Intellectual Property in the Deliverables shall vest in upon receipt by
the Consultant of payment in full for the Services. .
(b) Intellectual Property of existing at the Effective Date, including any
modification to such intellectual property, shall remain the property of
8 FURTHER PROPOSALS
8.1 If requested by the Consultant shall submit proposals for altering the
Services and the Fee shall be adjusted accordingly provided that such variations are
first agreed upon by the parties in writing.
8.2 If the parties cannot agree on any proposals for alteration to the Services, this
Agreement shall remain in full force and effect and neither party shall have any claim
against the other as a result of such proposed alteration to the Services.
9 PAYMENT
General
(a) In consideration of the provision of the Services in accordance with this
Agreement, will pay the Consultant the Fee. The Fee shall be paid in
the instalments shown in Part 6, the Schedule of Fees together with any costs
or expenses reimbursable pursuant to the Schedule of Particulars or clause
9.2(b) of this Agreement. If no Schedule of Fees is prescribed, the Consultant
shall be paid at the rates and times otherwise specified in the Schedule of
Particulars. The maximum Fee payable to the Consultant by under this
Agreement shall be that set out in the Schedule of Particulars (if any).

(b) It shall be a condition precedent to consideration of the Consultant’s
application for payment and the Consultant’s entitlement to payment under this
clause 9 that it shall have:
(i) submitted a report and any other information in accordance with clause
14.4 (where required); and
(ii) achieved any milestones by the dates identified in the Schedule of
Particulars (if any).
Full Remuneration
(a) Subject only where expressly provided otherwise, the Fee shall be full
remuneration to the Consultant for all costs, expenses or overheads incurred,
losses suffered or profit earned or to be earned in connection with the Services
and the performance of all of its other obligations under this Agreement.
(b) The Consultant shall be entitled to additional reimbursement at cost (excluding
overheads, depreciation and profit) for other expenses, not included in the Fee,
expressly authorised in writing by prior to their being incurred.
Additional Services
(a) If and the Consultant agree that the Consultant is to provide services
which are additional to the Services, such additional services shall (subject to
express agreement otherwise) either be charged on a lump sum, or time
charge, basis calculated using the rates set out in the Schedule of Particulars.
(b) The Consultant shall maintain up to date records for all reimbursable costs and
expenses incurred in providing such additional services, and in any other case
where Services are to be carried out on a time charge basis, and such records
shall clearly identify the relevant time incurred and work performed.
Request for Payment
(a) Within 5 working days of the expiry of one month after the Effective Date and
within 5 working days of the expiry of each month thereafter, the Consultant
may, subject to the provisions of the Schedule of Fees set out in the Schedule
of Particulars (if any), apply to for payment of an instalment of the Fee
and reimbursement of all costs and expenses for the Services provided during
the preceding month by submitting a tax invoice, for the amount due plus goods
and services tax, if any, charged in accordance with the Goods and Services
Tax Act 1985.
(b) Each such application shall:
(i) state the amount claimed in accordance with the Schedule of Fees;
(ii) where the invoice includes sums claimed under clause 9.2(b) or 9.3, be
supported by any vouchers, time-sheets, receipts and other information
which may be necessary to establish that such amounts are properly due
and payable under this Agreement; and

(iii) include a description of the Services provided in that month and their
value, and any other items requested by
Dispute in Payment
If disputes the accuracy of any of the Consultant’s invoice or wishes to dispute
any claim for underpayment or make a claim for overpayment, shall notify the
Consultant within 7 days of receipt of the invoice. If and the Consultant cannot
settle the dispute within 14 days of notification, then the dispute shall be
determined in accordance with clause 16. shall pay any undisputed amount
in accordance with this clause 9.
Date for Payment
shall pay all sums due to the Consultant in accordance with this Agreement
(less any amount disputed)by the 20th day of the following month from the date of the
Consultant’s valid tax invoice.
Mode of Payment
The Fee may be paid by electronic funds transfer to an account designated by the
Consultant for this purpose.
Currency
The Fee shall be paid in New Zealand dollars unless otherwise stated. No adjustment
shall be made to the Fee (or any element thereof) or any other payment on account of
any variation in any exchange rate.
Taxes
(a) shall pay, in addition to the Fee, all goods and services taxes levied in
respect of any supply of the Services.
(b) Subject to clause 9.10, the Consultant shall pay all other charges, levies, duties,
taxes or impositions payable in respect of the Services.
Withholding Tax
Clauses 9.10(a) to (f) below shall apply if, and to the extent that, any amount paid or
payable under this Agreement is an amount that is non resident withholding income or
a withholding payment, or another amount from which New Zealand tax Law requires
any amount to be withheld or deducted:
(a) shall be entitled to make any such deduction or withholding and shall
only be liable to pay the net amount to the Consultant.
(b) The amount of any such payment that is subject to a deduction or withholding
referred to in clause 9.10(a) shall be determined by the parties. However, the
Consultant is prima facie responsible for notifying of the appropriate
gross amount that is subject to such a deduction or withholding.
(c) If the amounts payable under this Agreement are withholding payments,
(specifically, but not limited to, payments to non resident contractors) the
Consultant may be entitled to exemptions or relief from the deductions or
Document Reference:

University of Auckland Consultancy Agreement
- 24 - Part 4
Terms and Conditions
withholding referred to in clause 9.10(a) above. Specifically, no deductions will
be made from payments made to the Consultant if:
(i) the Consultant delivers to a valid certificate of exemption issued
by the Inland Revenue before the payment is made that applies to the
relevant payment; or
(ii) the Consultant delivers to a statement on the Consultant’s
letterhead signed by an authorised representative that:
1. the Consultant has not and does not intend to have any employees
in New Zealand (in connection with the Services or any other
project) who in aggregate have not been present or will not be
present in New Zealand for 92 days or more in any 12 month period;
2. the Consultant has maintained a record of the number of days in
which its employees have performed services in New Zealand that
can be provided to if requested; and
3. the Consultant is eligible for relief from New Zealand income tax in
accordance with the double tax agreement between New Zealand
and the Consultant’s country of residence.
(d) The Consultant will ensure in relation to any payment received by it that the
correct amount of withholding has been made and, to the extent that it has not,
shall immediately notify to rectify the situation including refunding an
appropriate amount of the payment to meet the withholding tax liability
otherwise required to be made.
(e) If for whatever reason an incorrect amount of withholding has been deducted
and accounted for by then the Consultant shall pay to the full
amount of that withholding tax liability (including any penalties and/or use of
money interest) to put in the same after tax position it would have been
in had correctly deducted and accounted for that withholding.
(f) Clause 9.10(e) above does not apply to amounts (and only in relation to those
amounts) deducted by that have not been remitted by to the
Inland Revenue Department.
10 CHANGED CIRCUMSTANCES
Circumstances beyond the Control of the Consultant
(a) If circumstances arise following the date of execution of this Agreement for
which a party is not responsible and which are genuinely beyond its control and
which make it illegal or impossible for that party (“Affected Party”) to perform
its obligations in whole or in part in accordance with this Agreement, then the
Affected Party shall promptly notify the other party.
(b) The Affected Party shall not be liable for any failure to perform its obligations to
the extent the failure is caused by such circumstances, provided that this clause
will not apply to any:
(i) lack of funds or solvency issues of the Affected Party;
Document Reference:
Long Form Consultancy Services Agreement

University of Auckland Consultancy Agreement
- 25 - Part 4
Terms and Conditions
(ii) inclement weather conditions adversely affecting progress of the
obligations;
(iii) strikes, lock-outs or other industrial action;
(iv) failure by the Affected Party’s subcontractors or suppliers or the non-
availability or shortage of equipment, labour, utility services, goods or
materials; or
(v) event that makes the performance of the obligations more expensive or
difficult.
Duty to Mitigate
The Consultant shall exercise its best endeavours to avoid or remove the changed
circumstances, and to mitigate their effect.
Accrued Obligations
The existence of changed circumstances will not affect any rights or obligations which
may have accrued prior to such circumstances.
Right to Terminate
If any changed circumstances arise in terms of clause 10.1 and their effect continues
for a period of 120 days or more, may give to the Consultant notice of
termination, which shall take effect 20 days after the giving of the notice if at the end of
the 20 day period the effect continues.
11 WARRANTY AND LIABILITY

16 SETTLEMENT OF DISPUTES
Disputes
(a) Any dispute or difference of any kind whatsoever arising between and
the Consultant in connection with or arising out of this Agreement or of the
carrying out of the Services and whether during the provision of the Services or
after their completion and whether before or after the termination, abandonment
or breach of this Agreement (“a dispute”) shall be settled in accordance with the

(b) A dispute shall be deemed to arise when one party serves on the other a notice
(“a notice of dispute”) stating the nature of the dispute, together with brief
particulars of the facts and circumstances relied on by the party serving the
notice of dispute.
(c) Within 10 working days of the service of the notice of dispute the parties shall
meet, negotiate and seek to resolve the dispute in good faith.
Referral to CEOs
(a) If the parties are unable to resolve the dispute or make material progress
towards its resolution within 20 working days of the service of the notice of
dispute, then as a condition precedent to the referral of the dispute to arbitration
pursuant to clause 16.3, the dispute shall be referred to the chief executive
officers of and the Consultant for discussion and agreement.
(b) The chief executive officers shall meet within 10 working days after the expiry
of 20 working days of service of the notice of dispute and shall attempt to settle
the dispute.
(c) If either chief executive officer is not available, he or she may appoint a senior
manager to meet in their place pursuant to this clause, provided that any senior
manager appointed has not previously been involved in the dispute and has the
ability to enter into a binding agreement on behalf of the company.
Arbitration
(a) If the dispute is not resolved within 10 working days of when the dispute was
first referred to the chief executive officers or senior managers in accordance
with clause 16.2 then either the Consultant or may require the dispute
to be referred to arbitration.
(b) Pending the arbitrator’s decision, the parties shall continue to perform all their
obligations under this Agreement without prejudice.
